differences.title: 1. RENDERING APPROACH
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS updates the DOM directly without a virtual DOM for peak performance.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik resumes execution instantly by serializing and hydrating only necessary state.

differences.title: 2. REACTIVITY MODEL
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS uses fine-grained reactivity for efficient updates to specific DOM parts.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik leverages signals for reactive updates, inspired by SolidJS.

differences.title: 3. INITIAL LOAD
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS provides a very fast initial load due to its compilation.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's resumability leads to near-instantaneous initial loads, minimal JS on client.

differences.title: 4. CODE SPLITTING
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS handles code splitting automatically to optimize bundle sizes.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's resumability enables intelligent code splitting for critical rendering path.

differences.title: 5. LEARNING CURVE
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS concepts are familiar to React developers, making it easier to learn.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik's resumability is a novel concept, requiring a slight adjustment in thinking.

differences.title: 6. SERVER RENDERING
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS offers robust server-side rendering capabilities for SEO and performance.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik excels with SSR and its unique resumability for an optimized experience.

differences.title: 7. COMMUNITY SIZE
differences.leftDesc: SolidJS has a solid and growing community with active development.
differences.rightDesc: Qwik is newer, with an emerging community and strong potential.
